modules.title: Module 1: Introduction to ML
examFocus: Mitchell's well-posed learning definition (Task T, Performance P, Experience E)
Types of ML: supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement, semi-supervised
Basic data types: qualitative (nominal/ordinal) vs quantitative (interval/ratio)
Data pre-processing: handling missing values, outliers, noise, normalization, encoding

topics.title: Introduction, Well-Posed Problems & Types of ML
topics.summary: What ML is, Mitchell's T/P/E definition, and the main learning types.

topics.title: Applications, Tools, Problems Not to Solve & Issues in ML
topics.summary: Where ML is used, common tools/languages, and the key issues and limits of ML.

topics.title: ML Activities & Basic Types of Data
topics.summary: The ML modelling activities and the four measurement scales of data.

topics.title: Exploring Data, Data Quality & Pre-Processing
topics.summary: How to explore data, fix quality issues, and prepare data for modelling.

modules.title: Module 2: Feature Engineering
examFocus: Feature subset selection: filter vs wrapper vs embedded methods
Model validation: holdout, k-fold cross-validation, bootstrap
Confusion matrix metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, F1, specificity
Bias-variance tradeoff, overfitting vs underfitting

topics.title: Feature Transformation & Subset Selection
topics.summary: Creating useful features and choosing the best subset of features.

topics.title: Selecting & Training a Model
topics.summary: How to pick a model and split data correctly to train and validate it.

topics.title: Model Representation & Interpretability
topics.summary: How models represent learned knowledge and why interpretability matters.

topics.title: Evaluating & Improving Model Performance
topics.summary: Metrics to evaluate models and techniques to improve them.

modules.title: Module 3: Probability
examFocus: Frequentist vs Bayesian interpretation of probability
Common distributions: Bernoulli, Binomial, Normal
Bayes' theorem: prior, likelihood, posterior, MAP vs ML
Naive Bayes classifier with worked example

topics.title: Statistical Tools & Probability
topics.summary: Why statistics matter in ML and the two views of probability.

topics.title: Random Variables & Distributions
topics.summary: Random variables and the main discrete and continuous distributions.

topics.title: Bayesian Concept Learning & Estimation
topics.summary: Learning concepts the Bayesian way using prior, likelihood and posterior.

topics.title: Bayes' Theorem & Naive Bayes Classifier
topics.summary: Bayes' theorem and the Naive Bayes classifier with a worked example.

modules.title: Module 4: Supervised Learning
examFocus: Classification model and the supervised learning workflow
k-NN and Decision Trees (entropy, information gain, Gini index)
Random Forest (bagging) and SVM (hyperplane, margin, kernels)
Linear regression: least squares, MSE cost, R-squared

topics.title: Supervised Learning Intro & Classification Model
topics.summary: The basics of supervised learning and how classification models work.

topics.title: k-Nearest Neighbours & Decision Tree
topics.summary: Distance-based k-NN and tree-based learning with entropy and Gini.

topics.title: Random Forest & Support Vector Machines
topics.summary: Random Forest bagging and SVM hyperplanes, margins and kernels.

topics.title: Simple & Multiple Linear Regression
topics.summary: Fitting lines with least squares and measuring fit with MSE and R-squared.

modules.title: Module 5: Unsupervised Learning
examFocus: Supervised vs unsupervised learning differences
Clustering: k-means steps, hierarchical, DBSCAN
Association rules: support, confidence, lift and the Apriori principle
Learning theory: PAC learning, hypothesis space, version space, no-free-lunch

topics.title: Unsupervised Learning & Clustering
topics.summary: Clustering ideas and the k-means, hierarchical and DBSCAN algorithms.

topics.title: Association Rules & Apriori Algorithm
topics.summary: Finding item relationships using support, confidence, lift and Apriori.

topics.title: Learning Theory
topics.summary: Theory of learning: PAC, hypothesis/version space and no-free-lunch.
